title: GDPR Learning Track — EU (Regulation (EU) 2016/679)
verificationNote: Educational scaffold for the consulting team. Lessons cite Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (consolidated text from EUR-Lex), the EDPB Guidelines and Opinions, and the EDPS / national supervisory authority guidance where applicable. Material is comparative reference for cross-jurisdictional consulting work and is NEVER legal advice on EU obligations. For client-facing work involving EU exposure, engage EU-qualified counsel.
description: GDPR track mirroring the PDPA track structure (4 tiers + article deep-dives) so a consultant trained on one regime can pick up the other quickly. Each lesson cross-references the parallel PDPA lesson in ../pdpa/.
howToUse: Read tiers in order. Use article deep-dives when you need detail beyond what the tiered lessons cover. The cross-reference/pdpa-vs-gdpr.md document is the canonical side-by-side and should be read alongside T1.

tiers.name: Orientation
tiers.audience: Anyone new to GDPR — engineers, GRC professionals, executives, lawyers from adjacent practice areas.
tiers.outcome: Can name the 6 GDPR principles (Article 5) and the 8 data subject rights (Articles 15-22), explain the controller / processor / joint-controller distinction, articulate the lawful-basis hierarchy (Article 6), identify when an EU SA has jurisdiction, and quote GDPR penalty exposure (20M EUR / 4% global turnover).

lessons.title: Why GDPR matters and who it applies to
lessons.summary: GDPR territorial scope (Article 3 — establishment vs targeting), penalty exposure (Article 83 — 10M EUR / 2% or 20M EUR / 4%), the joint-controller and processor liability regime, the one-stop-shop mechanism and lead supervisory authority.
learningObjectives: State who Regulation 2016/679 binds (Article 3 — establishment + targeting + monitoring)
Quote the two penalty tiers (Article 83(4) and 83(5))
Identify the lead supervisory authority concept and OSS

lessons.title: GDPR Article 5 — the 6 principles in 15 minutes
lessons.summary: Lawfulness / fairness / transparency, purpose limitation, data minimisation, accuracy, storage limitation, integrity and confidentiality. Plus the accountability over-principle.
learningObjectives: Name all six Article 5(1) principles plus accountability (5(2))
Distinguish purpose limitation from compatibility assessment (5(1)(b) + 6(4))
State what accountability adds that PDPA's 7 Principles do not formally codify
Identify where each principle has a corresponding PDPA principle (cross-reference)

lessons.title: Article 6 lawful bases and Articles 15-22 data subject rights
lessons.summary: The six lawful bases (consent, contract, legal obligation, vital interests, public task, legitimate interests), the legitimate-interests three-part test, the eight data subject rights, and Article 22 (solely automated decisions).
learningObjectives: Recite the six Article 6(1) lawful bases
Distinguish Article 22 from general right to object (Article 21)
Map GDPR rights to PDPA equivalents (cross-reference)

lessons.title: EDPB Guidelines, EDPS Opinions, and CJEU case law — the citation hierarchy
lessons.summary: The hierarchy of GDPR authority: Regulation text > implementing acts > EDPB Guidelines > national SA guidance > CJEU case law > commentary. When to cite each. Why a finding citing a blog post will not survive review.
learningObjectives: Identify when CJEU case law overrides EDPB guidance (Schrems II as the canonical example)
Apply the issued-vs-draft distinction to EDPB Guidelines under consultation
Cite EDPB Guideline number + version when referencing

lessons.title: Checkpoint — GDPR orientation self-test
lessons.summary: Ten questions. If you can't answer eight, re-read the relevant lesson before moving to T2.

tiers.name: Practitioner
tiers.audience: New DPO or privacy professional taking operational responsibility for a GDPR programme.
tiers.outcome: Can build an Article 30 RoPA, draft Article 13/14 information notices, conduct an Article 35 DPIA, draft Article 33 / 34 breach notifications, and assess applicability of an Article 40 Code of Conduct.

lessons.title: Exercise — build an Article 30 RoPA from scratch
lessons.summary: Article 30(1) controller elements vs Article 30(2) processor elements. The micro-enterprise exception (Article 30(5)) and why it almost never applies. Common gaps.
lessons.deliverable: Article 30 RoPA covering 4 processing activities with both controller and processor entries

lessons.title: Exercise — draft Article 13 / 14 information notices
lessons.summary: Article 13 (direct collection) vs Article 14 (indirect collection); mandatory information elements; layered-notice patterns; EDPB transparency guidelines.
lessons.deliverable: Article 13 notice + Article 14 notice for a worked B2C platform

lessons.title: Exercise — Article 35 DPIA on a worked example
lessons.summary: When DPIA is mandatory (Article 35(3) + SA blacklists); the EDPB 9-criteria threshold; consultation with the SA under Article 36 where high residual risk remains.
lessons.deliverable: Article 35-compliant DPIA on an e-KYC scenario including Article 36 prior-consultation assessment

lessons.title: Exercise — Article 33 / 34 breach notifications
lessons.summary: Article 33: 72 hours to SA without undue delay; Article 34: high-risk-to-subject communication; the SA's expectation on harm-likelihood assessment.
lessons.deliverable: Article 33 SA notification + Article 34 subject communication for a worked ransomware scenario
citations: EDPB Guidelines on Personal Data Breach Notification

lessons.title: Article 40 Codes of Conduct and Article 42 Certification
lessons.summary: When sector codes apply; approval mechanism via SA / EDPB; certification as a transfer safeguard (Article 46(2)(f)) and as accountability evidence.
lessons.deliverable: Applicability assessment for 3 worked clients (cloud, AdTech, health)

lessons.title: Checkpoint — GDPR practitioner deliverables review
lessons.summary: Five GDPR artefacts you should now own. Compare side-by-side with the PDPA T2 outputs to see the structural difference between the two regimes.
lessons.deliverable: All 5 GDPR T2 deliverables reviewed; comparison memo against the PDPA T2 outputs

tiers.name: Programme Builder
tiers.audience: DPO standing up a GDPR programme or consultant leading client readiness.
tiers.outcome: Can stand up a GDPR programme across all controller obligations, design Chapter V cross-border architecture, and produce documentation defensible in a SA inspection.

lessons.title: GDPR programme scaffold — what every controller needs
lessons.summary: The controller obligation set: lawful basis register, RoPA, DPIA programme, breach response, DSAR programme, vendor / processor management, training, governance.
lessons.deliverable: 8-pillar programme plan with a 90-day sequencing for a B2C SaaS client

lessons.title: DPO under Articles 37-39 — when mandatory, what they do, who they report to
lessons.summary: Article 37 mandatory triggers; Article 38 position; Article 39 tasks; EDPB Guideline on DPOs; conflict-of-interest framework.
lessons.deliverable: DPO appointment memo + tasking + reporting line for a worked client

lessons.title: DPIA + Privacy by Design + Article 22 as a programme triad
lessons.summary: Article 35 DPIA + Article 25 Privacy by Design and by Default + Article 22 automated decisions. Documentation chain and overlap.
lessons.deliverable: Programme runbook integrating DPIA / PbD / Article 22 for a SaaS client using ML for risk scoring

lessons.title: GDPR Chapter V — cross-border transfer architecture
lessons.summary: Adequacy decisions; SCCs (2021 modular); BCRs; derogations (Article 49); Transfer Impact Assessments post-Schrems II; UK adequacy; pan-jurisdictional patterns.
lessons.deliverable: Cross-border transfer register + TIA + mechanism selection for a client transferring data to US / UK / Singapore / Malaysia

lessons.title: GDPR breach tabletop — Article 33 / 34 timing under pressure
lessons.summary: Scenario design; risk-of-harm assessment; the 72-hour clock starting point (awareness, not detection); communication-to-subject template; SA notification template per jurisdiction.
lessons.deliverable: Tabletop package + facilitator script + AAR template for a worked SaaS breach
citations: EDPB Guidelines 9/2022 on Personal Data Breach Notification

lessons.title: Checkpoint — GDPR programme readiness review
lessons.summary: Board-pack contents. Documentation set a SA inspection would ask for. Comparison against PDPA T3 board-pack.
lessons.deliverable: Defensible SA inspection pack + comparison memo with the PDPA T3 pack

tiers.name: Auditor / Assessor
tiers.audience: GDPR auditor, internal auditor leading a GDPR audit cycle.
tiers.outcome: Can perform a GDPR audit, write findings citing Article + section, and produce an audit pack defensible to the lead SA.

lessons.title: Anatomy of a GDPR audit pack
lessons.summary: Work programme structure; ISO 27701 as the controller / processor assurance reference; SOC 2 + Article 28 overlap.

lessons.title: Mapping articles to audit scope
lessons.summary: Controller obligations (Articles 5-25 + Chapter IV) as the test scope. Article 32 security as the largest single test surface. Article 24 accountability as the override.

lessons.title: EDPB Guidelines and CJEU case law — citation discipline
lessons.summary: Hierarchy of authority. CJEU > EDPB > national SA > commentary. Guideline version pinning. Schrems II as the canonical example of CJEU overriding established practice.

lessons.title: Writing a finding that maps to GDPR Article + EDPB Guideline
lessons.summary: Finding template; Article + sub-paragraph citation; EDPB Guideline cross-reference; the controller's accountability burden (Article 5(2)) as the residual finding category.
lessons.deliverable: Sample finding on an Article 13 information-notice gap, fully cited

lessons.title: Checkpoint — produce a GDPR finding
lessons.summary: Pick an Article. Walk through to a fully-formed finding. Self-grade against rubric and compare side-by-side with the parallel PDPA finding.
lessons.deliverable: Sample GDPR finding + side-by-side PDPA finding on the equivalent obligation

articleDeepDives.description: Reference deep-dives on each key GDPR Article. Use when you need detail beyond the tiered lessons. Each deep-dive includes scope, structure, common implementation patterns, EDPB / CJEU position, and the PDPA parallel.

deepDives.title: Article 5 — the six principles + accountability
deepDives.summary: Lawfulness/fairness/transparency, purpose limitation, data minimisation, accuracy, storage limitation, integrity and confidentiality + accountability.

deepDives.title: Articles 13 / 14 — information to be provided
deepDives.summary: Direct vs indirect collection; mandatory elements; layered notices; EDPB transparency guidelines.

deepDives.title: Article 22 — automated individual decision-making and profiling
deepDives.summary: The right not to be subject to solely-automated decisions; three exceptions; safeguards; sensitive-data overlay; WP29 Guidelines on Automated Individual Decision-Making.

deepDives.title: Article 25 — data protection by design and by default
deepDives.summary: PbD obligation, default-privacy obligation, EDPB Guidelines 4/2019.

deepDives.title: Article 30 — Records of Processing Activities
deepDives.summary: Controller vs processor elements; the micro-enterprise exception; common patterns.

deepDives.title: Articles 33 / 34 — breach notification
deepDives.summary: 72-hour SA notification + high-risk-to-subject communication; EDPB Guidelines 9/2022.

deepDives.title: Article 35 — Data Protection Impact Assessment
deepDives.summary: Mandatory triggers (Article 35(3)); EDPB 9-criteria threshold; SA blacklists; Article 36 prior consultation.

deepDives.title: Articles 37 / 38 / 39 — DPO appointment, position, tasks
deepDives.summary: Mandatory triggers; independence; tasks list; EDPB DPO Guidelines.

deepDives.title: Chapter V — international transfers (Articles 44-50)
deepDives.summary: Adequacy / SCCs (2021 modular) / BCRs / derogations; Schrems II; supplementary measures.
